member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.Extensions.EasyAEClientExtension.AcknowledgeMultipleConditions(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.EasyAEClient,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments[])

member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.
Conditions to be acknowledged.

overloads.remarks: The size of the input array will become the size of the output array. The element positions (indices) in the output array are the same as in the input array.
overloads.returns: The function returns an array of integer handles. Each such handle uniquely identifies the events subscription. The indices of elements in the output array are the same as those in the input array.

Array of arguments, one element per each OPC events specification involved in the operation.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEQualifiedNameTemplateAttribute.TemplateString
member.summary: The template string.

remarks.para: Null is allowed, because it allows to disable the template use from certain level onwards.

Following macros can be used in template strings:
$(ParentQualifiedName) The OPC qualified name of parent node.
$(BrowseName) The browse name of current node.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind
member.summary: Specifies how the OPC Alarms&Events event will be mapped.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Result
member.summary: Map the operation result, as or a derived type specific for the operation.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.ErrorCode
member.summary: Map the error code, as . Zero for success, negative values for errors, and positive values for warnings.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Exception
member.summary: Map the exception, as or a derived type. null if the operation has been successful.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.ErrorMessage
member.summary: Map the error message, as . An empty string if the operation has been successful.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.StatusInfo
member.summary: Map the status information, as .

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.QualifiedSourceName
member.summary: Map the source of event notification.
member.remarks: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Time
member.summary: Map the time of the event occurrence - for conditions, time that the condition transitioned into the new state or sub-condition.

remarks.para: For example, if the event notification is for acknowledgment of a condition, this would be the time that the condition became acknowledged.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Message
member.summary: Map the event notification message describing the event. This is the default mapping kind.
member.remarks: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.EventType
member.summary: Map indication of Simple, Condition-Related, or Tracking event.

remarks.para: One of constants.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.CategoryId
member.summary: Map event category ID.

remarks.para: Standard and Vendor-specific event category codes.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Severity
member.summary: Map the event severity (1..1000).
member.remarks: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.AttributeValues
member.summary: Map a dictionary of vendor-specific event attributes.

remarks.para: Attributes are vendor-specific.
If a server cannot provide reasonable data for an attribute, the returned VARIANT should be set to VT_EMPTY.
This is a dictionary where the key is attribute ID, and the value is the actual value of that event attribute.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Value
member.summary: Map vendor-specific event attribute, specified by its ID.

remarks.para: Attributes are vendor-specific.
If a server cannot provide reasonable data for an attribute, the returned VARIANT should be set to VT_EMPTY.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.ConditionName
member.summary: Map the name of the condition related to this event notification.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.SubconditionName
member.summary: Map the name of the current sub-condition, for multi-state conditions. For a single-state condition, this contains the condition name.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Enabled
member.summary: Map new state of the condition: The condition is enabled.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Active
member.summary: Map new state of the condition: The condition is active.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Acknowledged
member.summary: Map new state of the condition: The condition is acknowledged.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Quality
member.summary: Map the quality associated with the condition, as .

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: Values are as defined for the OPC Quality Flags in the OPC Data Access Server specification.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.AcknowledgeRequired
member.summary: Map a flag indicating that the related condition requires acknowledgment of this event.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The determination of those events which require acknowledgment is server specific. For example, transition into a LimitAlarm condition would likely require an acknowledgment, while the event notification of the resulting acknowledgment would likely not require an acknowledgment.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.ActiveTime
member.summary: Map time that the condition became active (for single-state conditions), or the time of the transition into the current sub-condition (for multi-state conditions).

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: This time is used by the client when acknowledging the condition.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.Cookie
member.summary: Map server defined cookie associated with the event notification.

para.b: This property is used only for Condition-Related Events (see EventType).

remarks.para: The cookie is used by the client when acknowledging the condition. This value is opaque to the client.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.LiveMapping.AEEventMappingKind.ActorId
member.summary: Map actor ID.

para.b: This property is used only for Tracking Events and for Condition-Related Events which are acknowledgment notifications (see EventType).

remarks.para: For tracking events, this is the actor ID for the event notification.
For condition-related events, this is the AcknowledgerID when the new state is acknowledged. If the AcknowledgerID is an empty string, the event was automatically acknowledged by the server.
For other events, the value is an empty string.
The mapping target value is not changed if the operation resulted in an exception.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments
member.summary: Holds arguments need to perform OPC-A&E condition acknowledgement operation.

members.member: Default value of the property. Equals to the type name in curly barckets.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.DefaultComment
member.summary: Default comment. An empty string.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.DefaultConditionName
member.summary: Default condition name. An empty string.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.#ctor

overloads.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with default data.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.#ctor(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments)
member.param: Arguments to copy from.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.#ctor(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AENodeDescriptor,System.String,System.DateTime,System.Int64,System.String,System.String)
member.param: The OPC server involved in the operation.
Contains fully qualified source name. Identifies the source of the condition that is being acknowledged, e.g. FIC101.

A condition name, identifying the condition that is being acknowledged. Condition Names are unique within the scope of the event server. Examples of Condition Names might be "LevelAlarm" or "Deviation".

An active time corresponding to the Source and Condition Name pair. This parameter uniquely identifies a specific transition of the condition to the active state or into a different sub-condition and is the same as the .SubconditionActiveTime condition attribute. Active Times are passed to the client in the event notification. If the condition has become active again or transitioned into a different sub-condition at a later time, this acknowledgment will be ignored.

A server supplied "cookie" corresponding to the Source and Condition Name pair that in addition to the Active Time uniquely identifies a specific event notification. Cookies are passed to the client in the event notification. The client is responsible for returning the same cookie parameter, received in the event notification, back to the server in the condition acknowledgment.

A string passed in by the client, identifying who is acknowledging the condition. This is an attribute (AcknowledgerID) of the condition that identifies who acknowledged the condition. This is just a string generated by the client. This is also also included as the ActorId in the acknowledgment event notification sent to all subscribing clients. When omitted, a value is used. An AcknowledgerId cannot result in an empty string, since such AcknowledgerId is used to indicate that the event was automatically acknowledged by the server.
Comment string passed in by the client associated with acknowledging the condition. An empty string indicating no comment is allowed. When omitted, an empty string is used instead.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.#ctor(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AENodeDescriptor,System.String,System.DateTime,System.Int64,System.String)
member.param: The OPC server involved in the operation.
Contains fully qualified source name. Identifies the source of the condition that is being acknowledged, e.g. FIC101.

A condition name, identifying the condition that is being acknowledged. Condition Names are unique within the scope of the event server. Examples of Condition Names might be "LevelAlarm" or "Deviation".

An active time corresponding to the Source and Condition Name pair. This parameter uniquely identifies a specific transition of the condition to the active state or into a different sub-condition and is the same as the .SubconditionActiveTime condition attribute. Active Times are passed to the client in the event notification. If the condition has become active again or transitioned into a different sub-condition at a later time, this acknowledgment will be ignored.

A server supplied "cookie" corresponding to the Source and Condition Name pair that in addition to the Active Time uniquely identifies a specific event notification. Cookies are passed to the client in the event notification. The client is responsible for returning the same cookie parameter, received in the event notification, back to the server in the condition acknowledgment.

A string passed in by the client, identifying who is acknowledging the condition. This is an attribute (AcknowledgerID) of the condition that identifies who acknowledged the condition. This is just a string generated by the client. This is also also included as the ActorId in the acknowledgment event notification sent to all subscribing clients. When omitted, a value is used. An AcknowledgerId cannot result in an empty string, since such AcknowledgerId is used to indicate that the event was automatically acknowledged by the server.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.#ctor(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AENodeDescriptor,System.String,System.DateTime,System.Int64)
member.param: The OPC server involved in the operation.

Active Times are passed to the client in the event notification. If the condition has become active again or transitioned into a different sub-condition at a later time, this acknowledgment will be ignored.

A server supplied "cookie" corresponding to the Source and Condition Name pair that in addition to the Active Time uniquely identifies a specific event notification. Cookies are passed to the client in the event notification. The client is responsible for returning the same cookie parameter, received in the event notification, back to the server in the condition acknowledgment.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAcknowledgeConditionArguments.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.DateTime,System.Int64,System.String,System.String)
member.param: Name of the machine. Determines the computer on which the OPC server is located. May be an empty string, in which case the OPC server is regarded to exist on the local computer or at the computer specified for it by DCOM configuration.
Contains ProgID of the OPC server.
Fully qualified source name. Identifies the source of the condition that is being acknowledged, e.g. FIC101.

A condition name, identifying the condition that is being acknowledged. Condition Names are unique within the scope of the event server. Examples of Condition Names might be "LevelAlarm" or "Deviation".

An active time corresponding to the Source and Condition Name pair. This parameter uniquely identifies a specific transition of the condition to the active state or into a different sub-condition and is the same as the .SubconditionActiveTime condition attribute. Active Times are passed to the client in the event notification. If the condition has become active again or transitioned into a different sub-condition at a later time, this acknowledgment will be ignored.

A server supplied "cookie" corresponding to the Source and Condition Name pair that in addition to the Active Time uniquely identifies a specific event notification. Cookies are passed to the client in the event notification. The client is responsible for returning the same cookie parameter, received in the event notification, back to the server in the condition acknowledgment.

A string passed in by the client, identifying who is acknowledging the condition. This is an attribute (AcknowledgerID) of the condition that identifies who acknowledged the condition. This is just a string generated by the client. This is also also included as the ActorId in the acknowledgment event notification sent to all subscribing clients. When omitted, a value is used. An AcknowledgerId cannot result in an empty string, since such AcknowledgerId is used to indicate that the event was automatically acknowledged by the server.
Comment string passed in by the client associated with acknowledging the condition. An empty string indicating no comment is allowed. When omitted, an empty string is used instead.

member.param: The OPC server involved in the operation.
Subscription parameters to be used when the subscription is created

When set, the subscription will automatically perform a Refresh after each successful connection to the server (either the initial connection, or any automatic reconnection after the connection is lost).
The state object (can be any value supplied by your code); available in event notifications.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEEventsSubscriptionArguments.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Object,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AESubscriptionFilter,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.AlarmsAndEvents.AEAttributeSetDictionary,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.param: Name of the machine. Determines the computer on which the OPC server is located. May be an empty string, in which case the OPC server is regarded to exist on the local computer or at the computer specified for it by DCOM configuration.
Contains ProgID of the OPC server.

The requested notification rate. The notification rate is in milliseconds and tells the server how often to send event notifications. This is a minimum time - do not send event notifications any faster that this UNLESS the buffer size is reached. A value of 0 for notification rate means that the server should send event notifications as soon as it gets them. This parameter is used to improve communications efficiency between client and server. This parameter is a recommendation from the client, and the server is allowed to ignore the parameter.
The state object (can be any value supplied by your code); available in event notifications.
An that determines the filtering criteria to be used for the event subscription.

Determines the attributes to be returned for each category with event notifications. A dictionary where they key is the category ID, and the value is an array of attribute IDs for that event category.
Creates activated or deactivated subscription. See ChangeEventSubscription for more information.

When set, the subscription will automatically perform a Refresh after each successful connection to the server (either the initial connection, or any automatic reconnection after the connection is lost).

This is a minimum time - do not send event notifications any faster that this UNLESS the buffer size is reached. A value of 0 for notification rate means that the server should send event notifications as soon as it gets them. This parameter is used to improve communications efficiency between client and server. This parameter is a recommendation from the client, and the server is allowed to ignore the parameter.
A callback method to be invoked for each OPC event.
The state object (can be any value supplied by your code); available in event notifications.
An that determines the filtering criteria to be used for the event subscription.

member.summary: Default value of the property. Equals to false.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveBinding.DABindingGroup.DefaultAutoRead
member.summary: Default value of the property. Equals to false.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveBinding.DABindingGroup.DefaultAutoSubscribe
member.summary: Default value of the property. Equals to true.

member.name: F:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveBinding.DABindingGroup.DefaultAutoWrite
member.summary: Default value of the property. Equals to false.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveBinding.DABindingGroup.ExecuteGet
member.summary: Executes the "Get" operation on all bindings in this binding group.
member.remarks: The "Get" operation obtains value of an OPC property and stores it into the mapping source. Only bindings of type are affected; other bindings are ignored.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveBinding.DABindingGroup.ExecuteRead
member.summary: Executes the "Read" operation on all bindings in this binding group.
member.remarks: The "Read" operation reads a value of an OPC item and stores it into the mapping source. Only bindings of type that include in their are affected; other bindings are ignored.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveBinding.DABindingGroup.ExecuteSubscribe(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Executes the "Subscribe" operation on all bindings in this binding group.
member.param: true to subscribe; false to unsubscribe.
member.remarks: The "Subscribe" operation subscribes to or unsubscribes from changes of an OPC item. Only bindings of type that include in their are affected; other bindings are ignored.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveBinding.DABindingGroup.ExecuteWrite
member.summary: Executes the "Write" operation on all bindings in this binding group.
member.remarks: The "Write" operation writes a value from the mapping source into an OPC item. Only bindings of type that include in their are affected; other bindings are ignored.

This error should not happen unless you explicitly specify the values in mapping context, because the default mapping context contains de [rest of string was truncated]";.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveMapping.DAReadAttribute.ValueAge
member.summary: Determines the age of value you would like to obtain.

remarks.para: This value is significant only when the property is set to ByValueAge.
The value must be greater than or equal to zero, or it must be equal to .
When the value is , the value will always be provided from the memory, i.e. without actually accessing the OPC server.

member.summary: Fastest update rate used when the design is made online.

In order to maintain responsiveness, the update rate is limited.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension
member.summary: Contains extension methods to the class.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension.ReadItem``1(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.EasyDAClient,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeDescriptor)

overloads.summary: Reads a typed named item from an OPC server. A typed value, quality and timestamp are returned.

remarks.para: The server can be local or can be remotely accessed via DCOM. Optionally, an access path can be specified or a specific data type can be requested.
The ReadItem method only waits for the first update from the server (or until the timeout elapses); it does not wait until the quality becomes "uncertain" or "good".

member.summary: Reads a typed named item using descriptor objects for the OPC server and OPC-DA item.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.
The OPC server involved in the operation.
The OPC item involved in the operation.
member.returns: If successful, the function returns a object. The object contains the value, timestamp and quality of the OPC item requested.
member.exception: Thrown when the OPC operation fails.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension.ReadItem``1(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.EasyDAClient,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAReadParameters)
member.summary: Reads a typed named item using descriptor objects for the OPC server and OPC-DA item, and specifying read parameters such as the data source or value age.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.
The OPC server involved in the operation.
The OPC item involved in the operation.
Read parameters, such as the data source or value age.
member.returns: If successful, the function returns a object. The object contains the value, timestamp and quality of the OPC item requested.
member.exception: Thrown when the OPC operation fails.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension.ReadItem``1(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.EasyDAClient,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Reads a typed named item using individual parameters specifying its location, and a specific data type.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.

Name of the machine. Determines the computer on which the OPC server is located. May be an empty string, in which case the OPC server is regarded to exist on the local computer or at the computer specified for it by DCOM configuration.
Contains ProgID of the OPC server to read from.
Contains OPC item identifier.
member.returns: If successful, the function returns a object. The object contains the value, timestamp and quality of the OPC item requested.
member.exception: Thrown when the OPC operation fails.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension.ReadItem``1(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.EasyDAClient,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads a typed named item using individual parameters specifying its location, and value age.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.

Name of the machine. Determines the computer on which the OPC server is located. May be an empty string, in which case the OPC server is regarded to exist on the local computer or at the computer specified for it by DCOM configuration.
Contains ProgID of the OPC server to read from.
Contains OPC item identifier.
The age of value you would like to obtain.
member.returns: If successful, the function returns a object. The object contains the value, timestamp and quality of the OPC item requested.
member.exception: Thrown when the OPC operation fails.

remarks.para: The data source (a device, OPC server's cache, or a value from OPC client component memory cache) is dynamically determined based on the argument.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension.ReadItemValue``1(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.EasyDAClient,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeDescriptor)

overloads.summary: Reads a typed named item from an OPC server. Only the item's typed value is returned (quality and timestamp are not returned).

remarks.para: The server can be local or can be remotely accessed via DCOM. Optionally, an access path can be specified or a specific data type can be requested.

Use this method if you are only interested in the actual typed value of the OPC item. Use ReadItem method if you also need the quality or timestamp. The ReadItemValue method makes it very easy to obtain the actual typed data value with just one function call.
The ReadItemValue method waits until the quality becomes "good", or the timeout elapses.

member.summary: Reads a value of typed named item using descriptor objects for the OPC server and OPC-DA item.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.
The OPC server involved in the operation.
The OPC item involved in the operation.
member.exception: Thrown when the OPC operation fails.
member.returns: If successful, the function returns the actual, typed value of OPC item requested.

member.summary: Reads a value of named OPC item, and converts it to an array of . The value is requested from the OPC server using a corresponding data type.

Name of the machine. Determines the computer on which the OPC server is located. May be an empty string, in which case the OPC server is regarded to exist on the local computer or at the computer specified for it by DCOM configuration.
Contains ProgID of the OPC server to read from.
Contains OPC item identifier.
The value to be written.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension.GetItemPropertyRecord(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.EasyDAClient,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeDescriptor)

overloads.summary: Obtains a structure filled with OPC property values for a given OPC item.

member.summary: Obtains a structure filled with OPC property values of all well-known OPC properties for a given OPC item.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.
The OPC server involved in the operation.
The descriptor of the OPC node involved in the operation.
member.returns: Returns a structure containing the OPC property values requested.

remarks.p: This method allows you to obtain a structure filled in with property values for a given OPC item. It can retrieve all well-known properties at once, or you can pass in a set of property Ids that you are interested in. You can then simply use the properties in the resulting structure, without further looking up the values in any way.
Using implicit conversions, you can pass an item ID in place of node descriptor.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.Extensions.EasyDAClientExtension.GetItemPropertyRecord(OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.EasyDAClient,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.ServerDescriptor,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DANodeDescriptor,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.DAPropertyId},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Obtains a structure filled with OPC property values for a given OPC item. You can specify the property Ids.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.
The OPC server involved in the operation.
The descriptor of the OPC node involved in the operation.
Specifies which OPC properties should be obtained and returned.
If false, any failures cause the method to throw an exception. If true, failures are ignored, and the affected OPC properties will contain default values.
member.returns: Returns a structure containing the OPC property values requested.

remarks.p: This method allows you to obtain a structure filled in with property values for a given OPC item. It can retrieve all well-known properties at once, or you can pass in a set of property Ids that you are interested in. You can then simply use the properties in the resulting structure, without further looking up the values in any way.
Using implicit conversions, you can pass an item ID in place of node descriptor.

overloads.summary: Obtains a filled with OPC property values for a given OPC item.

member.summary: Obtains a dictionary filled with all well-known OPC property values for a given OPC item.
member.param: The client object that will perform the operation.
The OPC server involved in the operation.
The descriptor of the OPC node involved in the operation.
member.returns: Returns a dictionary of objects keyed by .

remarks.p:
This method allows you to obtain a dictionary of property values for a given OPC item, where a key to the dictionary is the property Id. You can pass in a set of property Ids that you are interested in, or have the method obtain all well-known OPC properties. You can then easily extract the value of any property by looking it up in a dictionary (as opposed to having to numerically index into an array, as with the base
method).
Using implicit conversions, you can pass an item ID in place of node descriptor.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveMapping.DAItemIdTemplateAttribute.TemplateString
member.summary: The template string.

remarks.para: Null is allowed, because it allows to disable the template use from certain level onwards.

Following macros can be used in template strings:
$(ParentItemId) The OPC Item Id of parent node.
$(BrowseName) The browse name of current node.

member.summary: Denotes that the member should be involved in OPC Data Access mapping.

member.summary: Gives information about OPC Data Access node.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveMapping.DANodeAttribute.BrowsePath
member.summary: Specifies the browse path for the OPC node.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveMapping.DANodeAttribute.Deferred
member.summary: Determines whether the node will be mapped in a deferred manner.
member.remarks: When false, the member will be set to the mapped object itself; when true, the member will be set to a function that returns the mapped object (deferred mapping). The must be set to provide the deferred mapping function for the given mapping context.

member.summary: Executes the "Get" operation on all mappings in this mapper.
member.remarks: The "Get" operation obtains value of an OPC property and stores it into the mapping source. Only mappings of type are affected; other mappings are ignored.

member.summary: Executes the "Read" operation on all mappings in this mapper.

member.summary: Executes the "Subscribe" operation on all mappings in this mapper.
member.param: true to subscribe; false to unsubscribe.
member.remarks: The "Subscribe" operation subscribes to or unsubscribes from changes of an OPC item. Only mappings of type that include in their are affected; other mappings are ignored.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveMapping.DAMapper.DefaultClient
member.summary: A default client object.

remarks.para: The default client object is used when no client object is specified in the constructor of the mapper.
The initial value is equal to ; all its limitations apply.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveMapping.DAMapper.SharedInstance
member.summary: A default, shared instance of the mapper object.

remarks.para: Use this property with care, as its usability is limited. Its main use is for testing, and for non-library application code where just a single instance is sufficient.
The default instance is not suitable for Windows Forms or similar environments, where a specific may be used with each form.

We also do not recommend using the default instance for library code, due to conflicts that may arise if your library sets some instance parameters which may not be the same as what other libraries or the final application expects.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.DataAccess.LiveMapping.DAMappingProvider.SharedInstance
member.summary: A default, shared instance of the mapping provider object.

remarks.para: Use this property with care, as its usability is limited. Its main use is for testing, and for non-library application code where just a single instance is sufficient.
The default instance is not suitable for Windows Forms or similar environments, where a specific may be used with each form.

We also do not recommend using the default instance for library code, due to conflicts that may arise if your library sets some instance parameters which may not be the same as what other libraries or the final application expects.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.ISwtbOPCDABasic
member.summary: The ISwtbOPCDABasic interface provides an easy to use, yet powerful interface primarily for OPC based communications, although not entirely restricted to OPC based technology.

remarks.para: This interface provides capabilities for reading and writing to one or more data-points in any location. The intent behind this interface is to provide a completely agnostic means of retrieving data, as well as storing data.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.ISwtbOPCDABasic.BrowseBranches(System.String,System.String,System.String[]@,System.String[]@)

overloads.summary: Browses the branches within a server.

member.summary: Browses the branches within a server.
member.param: The computer to connect to
The server to connect to
The fully-qualified branch-names found at the root level
the names of the branches found at the root level

remarks.para: This method allows you to retrieve a list of branches available within a server.

This specific method will ONLY retrieve a list of branches from the root of the server. If you need to access a level lower than the root, then you will need to use an overloaded equivalent of this method.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.ISwtbOPCDABasic.Read(System.String,System.String,System.String,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.DataLocation,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads an item, specifing the Common.DataLocation location, and mandating a maximum amount of time the transaction should be allowed in order to complete.
member.param: The computer where the data-source is located.
The name of the software we wish to use/enquire.
The item to use
The data-source Common.DataLocation to read the value from.
The maximum amount of time to allow the transaction to complete.
member.returns: The value of the reading

remarks.para: This read implementation provides you with more control over the read. You can specify the server, item and the physical location from where the data should be read, but you can also specify a timeout setting which defines the maximum amount of time the transaction is permitted to take before failing.
Note: The Common.DataLocation option may not be supported by all implementations. Note: The Timeout option may not be supported by all implementations.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.ISwtbOPCDABasic.ReadAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.AsyncComplete,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.DataLocation,System.Int32,System.Object)

overloads.summary: Reads a number of items asynchronously, while allowing you to specify the location from where the data should be read, and providing a maximum amount of time allowed for the transaction to complete.

member.summary: Reads a value asynchronously, allowing you to choose the location from where the data should be read, as well as specifying the maximum amount of time allowed for this operation.
member.param: The computer where the data-source is located.
The name of the software we wish to use/enquire.
The item to use
The method to invoke when the transaction is complete.
The data-source Common.DataLocation to read the value from.
The maximum amount of time to allow the transaction to complete.
A miscellanous object to pass into the method, that will be passed to the callback method when the transaction completes.

remarks.para: Asynchronous reads allow you to invoke a read, but do not require your code to "block" while that read takes place. For example, if you invoke a read that is NOT asynchronous, then the line of code that you invoked the method on, is actually blocked until the call completes. With an Asynchronous read, your code will continue processing while the read processes in the background. Then, when the read is complete, a user-specified method will be invoked which is where you will receive the results of the read.
This method allows you to specify from where the data should be read from within the server. See Common.DataLocation for more information.
You may specify a maximum amount of time this transaction is permitted in order to complete. This functionality may not be available in all implementations of this interface.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.ISwtbOPCDABasic.ReadAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.AsyncComplete,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.DataLocation,System.Object)
member.summary: Reads an item asynchronously, while allowing you to specify the location from where the data will be read.
member.param: The computer where the data-source is located.
The name of the software we wish to use/enquire.
The item to use
The method to invoke when the transaction is complete.
The data-source Common.DataLocation to read the value from.
A miscellanous object to pass into the method, that will be passed to the callback method when the transaction completes.

remarks.para: Asynchronous reads allow you to invoke a read, but do not require your code to "block" while that read takes place. For example, if you invoke a read that is NOT asynchronous, then the line of code that you invoked the method on, is actually blocked until the call completes. With an Asynchronous read, your code will continue processing while the read processes in the background. Then, when the read is complete, a user-specified method will be invoked which is where you will receive the results of the read.
This method allows you to specify from where the data should be read from within the server. See Common.DataLocation for more information.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.ISwtbOPCDABasic.WriteAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Object,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.AsyncComplete,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.DataLocation,System.Int32,System.Object)

overloads.summary: Writes values to multiple items, asynchronously.

member.summary: Writes a value asynchronously.
member.param: The computer where the data-source is located.
The name of the software we wish to use/enquire.
The item to use
The value to write.
The method to invoke when the transaction is complete.
The data-source Common.DataLocation to read the value from.
The maximum amount of time to allow the transaction to complete.
A miscellanous object to pass into the method, that will be passed to the callback method when the transaction completes.

remarks.para: When you have multiple items to write to the server, it may be beneficial in some cases (but not all cases) to make the call asynchronous. This allows your code to continue executing while the write occurs in the background. When the overall transaction is complete, a method that you can specify will be invoked. The method will receive arguments informing you of any errors that occurred, along with the results of each and every write instruction.
This method allows you to specify from where the data should be read from within the server. See Common.DataLocation for more information.
You may specify a maximum amount of time this transaction is permitted in order to complete. This functionality may not be available in all implementations of this interface.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.ITagVTQ.Quality
member.summary: Quality of this data

remarks.para: This is primarily an OPC function.

Typical OPC Function codes
0 = BAD Quality
128 = UNCERTAIN quality
192 = GOOD Quality
The quality is a 16-bit word where all 16 bits each represent various quality codes.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.NamespaceDoc
member.summary: This namespace contains classes that serve as a replacement for Software Toolbox Extender (www.opcextender.net) component.

member.name: T:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.NetworkBrowser
member.summary:
Provides a mechanism for supplying a list of all PC names in the local network.
This class makes use of a DllImport instruction. The purpose of which is as follows: When a DllImport declaration is made in managed code (C#) it is a call to a legacy unmanaged code module, normally a C++ Dynamic Link Library. These C++ Dll's are usually part of the operating system API, or some other vendors API, and must be used to carry out operations that are not native within the managed code C# framework. This is fairly normal within the windows world. The only thing that needs careful consideration is the construction of the correct type of STRUCTS, object pointers, and attribute markers, which all contribute to making the link between managed (C#) and unmanaged code (C++) more seamless
This class makes use of the following Dll calls
Netapi32.dll : NetServerEnum, The NetServerEnum function lists all servers of the specified type that are visible in a domain. For example, an application can call NetServerEnum to list all domain controllers only or all SQL servers only. You can combine bit masks to list several types. For example, a value of 0x00000003 combines the bit masks for SV_TYPE_WORKSTATION (0x00000001) and SV_TYPE_SERVER (0x00000002).
Netapi32.dll : NetApiBufferFree, The NetApiBufferFree function frees the memory that the NetApiBufferAllocate function allocates. Call NetApiBufferFree to free the memory that other network management functions return.

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.NetworkBrowser.GetNetworkComputers
member.summary: Uses the DllImport : NetServerEnum with all its required parameters (see http://msdn.microsoft.com/library/default.asp?url=/library/en-us/netmgmt/netmgmt/netserverenum.asp for full details or method signature) to retrieve a list of domain SV_TYPE_WORKSTATION and SV_TYPE_SERVER PC's
member.returns: Arraylist that represents all the SV_TYPE_WORKSTATION and SV_TYPE_SERVER PC's in the Domain

member.name: M:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.OPCDataNETEngine.ReadAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.AsyncComplete,OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.Common.DataLocation,System.Int32,System.Object)

overloads.summary: Reads a number of items asynchronously, while allowing you to specify the location from where the data should be read, and providing a maximum amount of time allowed for the transaction to complete.

member.summary: Reads a value asynchronously, allowing you to choose the location from where the data should be read, as well as specifying the maximum amount of time allowed for this operation.
member.param: The computer where the data-source is located.
The name of the software we wish to use/enquire.
The item to use
The method to invoke when the transaction is complete.
The data-source Common.DataLocation to read the value from.
The maximum amount of time to allow the transaction to complete.
A miscellanous object to pass into the method, that will be passed to the callback method when the transaction completes.

remarks.para: Asynchronous reads allow you to invoke a read, but do not require your code to "block" while that read takes place. For example, if you invoke a read that is NOT asynchronous, then the line of code that you invoked the method on, is actually blocked until the call completes. With an Asynchronous read, your code will continue processing while the read processes in the background. Then, when the read is complete, a user-specified method will be invoked which is where you will receive the results of the read.
This method allows you to specify from where the data should be read from within the server. See Common.DataLocation for more information.
You may specify a maximum amount of time this transaction is permitted in order to complete. This functionality may not be available in all implementations of this interface.

remarks.para: When you have multiple items to write to the server, it may be beneficial in some cases (but not all cases) to make the call asynchronous. This allows your code to continue executing while the write occurs in the background. When the overall transaction is complete, a method that you can specify will be invoked. The method will receive arguments informing you of any errors that occurred, along with the results of each and every write instruction.
This method allows you to specify from where the data should be read from within the server. See Common.DataLocation for more information.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.OPCDataNETEngine.DefaultDeadband
member.summary: The requested Deadband that the subscription should be applied with.

remarks.para: Deadband is a % of fluctuation within the Tag data-change notifications. The percentage is actually on the full-range of the data-type of the tag.

While the deadband concept itself is quite simple and easy to understand, the the implementation is actually quite widely misunderstood. For a deadband to work effectively, it is important to understand how it works in the first place.

A deadband is a "tolerance" in data that you wish to accept. For example, if monitoring the temperature of a freezer, the temperature may fluctuate by a 0.1 of a degree, but you may only want to know when the temperature has changed by half of a degree (0.5). This is a prime candidate for a dead band.

A deadband must first of all know where its "Bounds" are, i.e. its upper and lower bound. In the case of a freezer, we could simply say 0 to 100 (for degrees Celcius). Then the deadband value is the fluctuation within these bounds, in our case we only wan to know when the value changes by a half of a degree C, which would mean a deadband of 0.5 will be used.

In some cases, no upper/lower bounds are defined for an item. If the server where the item resides does not provide these bounds (often referred to as "scaling") then this topic becomes quite confusing...

para.b: Dealing with Deadbands that have no upper/lower Bounds defined

remarks.para: This is where things get a little complicated... because no upper or lower bounds are specified, they will actually be "assumed" by the server. How does the server "assume" the upper/lower bounds? by looking at the data-type of the item and using the upper and lower bounds of the data-type, for example:

Examples of upper/lower bounds of standard data-types
Integer = -2147483648 to +2147483647
Short = -32,768 to +32,767
Long = -9,223,372,036,854,775,808 to +9,223,372,036,854,775,807

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.OPCDataNETEngine.DefaultScanRate

member.value: The default scan-rate to use when none is specified. Valid ranges are from 100 to 60000 (times in msec).

remarks.para: The default scanrate is used when new bindings/relationships are created and/or when no scanrate has been specified. The default scanrate is in milliseconds (msec). The allowed range of values is between 100 and 60000.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.TagVTQ.Quality
member.summary: Quality of this data

remarks.para: This is primarily an OPC function.

Typical OPC Function codes
0 = BAD Quality
128 = UNCERTAIN quality
192 = GOOD Quality
The quality is a 16-bit word where all 16 bits each represent various quality codes.

member.name: P:OpcLabs.EasyOpc.SwtbExtenderReplacement.TagVTQ.Timestamp
member.summary: Timestamp of when the value was obtained

remarks.para: This property indicates the age of the Value value. Each time the Value property changes, this timestamp will be updated accordingly.
